title: Explicit heat grid step preserves constant grid states

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a constant grid state, the shifted three-point explicit heat update returns the same constant value at every natural-number index.
assumptions: r and c are real numbers
i is a natural-number grid index
the grid state is constant with value c
informal.expected_conclusion: The heat grid update preserves a constant grid state.

declarations.name: heatStepGrid
declarations.statement: def heatStepGrid (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := heatStep r (u i) (u (i + 1)) (u (i + 2))

declarations.name: heatStepGrid_const
declarations.statement: theorem heatStepGrid_const (r c : Real) (i : Nat) : heatStepGrid r (fun _ => c) i = c

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: For a constant grid state, the shifted three-point explicit heat update returns the same constant value at every natural-number index.
tasks.target: theorem heatStepGrid_const (r c : Real) (i : Nat) : heatStepGrid r (fun _ => c) i = c

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding heatStepGrid and reusing heatStep_const.

tasks.input: Find the scalar heat theorem used to prove constant-state preservation for the grid-window update.
